message:
fix(h2): prevent last_stream_id pollution on rejected HEADERS (asupersync-32jl1)

process_headers() called track_stream_id() before get_or_create() validation.
If get_or_create rejected a stream (wrong parity, non-monotonic ID), last_stream_id
was already bumped, causing GOAWAY to advertise a higher last_stream_id than actually
processed — violating RFC 7540 §6.8.

Fix: validate via get_or_create() in a scope block before tracking. Re-borrow via
get_mut() for subsequent operations (mirrors the pattern in process_data()).

Regression test: headers_on_wrong_parity_stream_does_not_pollute_last_stream_id

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
